name: cable rope hammer preacher curl
instructions: Attach a rope attachment to a low pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Position your upper arms against the preacher bench pad, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the rope towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the rope back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Attach a rope attachment to a low pulley cable machine. Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Position your upper arms against the preacher bench pad, keeping your elbows slightly bent. Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the rope towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps. Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps. Inhale and slowly lower the rope back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
